title: fix: Use trustline balance direction to validate IOU PaymentMint/PaymentBurn
body:

## High Level Overview of Change

<!--
Please include a summary of the changes.
This may be a direct input to the release notes.
If too broad, please consider splitting into multiple PRs.
If a relevant task or issue, please link it here.
-->

**Change 1:**

PaymentMint/PaymentBurn granular permission check for IOU relies on:
- Trust limit (destLimit > 0 / accountLimit > 0): identifies which side is willing to hold another side's IOU
- Balance direction (accountIsHolder): confirms which direction DirectStepI will actually execute (issue vs redeem)

MPT does not have this issue because MPTokenIssuanceID encodes the issuer unambiguously, there is no endpoint aliasing where either side of the trustline can appear as the issuer.


**Change 2:** 
in Permission::permissionToTxType, force the permission value within range [1, 65536]

title: chore: Enable groups of clang-tidy checks by default
body:

the branch after opening the PR will close the PR.
https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/managing-branches-in-your-repository/renaming-a-branch
-->

## High Level Overview of Change

Before, we had 4 comments why some particular check is not enabled.
It gave wrong impression we're covered well by tidy checks.
**In reality, there are 140!!!! checks we missed**

We should either enable them, or explicitly decide we don't need them.
The practical effect going forward: future clang-tidy upgrades will **auto-enable any new checks** added to those six groups

Nits:
- `llvm-namespace-comment` is left as a single change, I don't think they're generally meant to be run on other codebases
- I also don't enable any other groups for now, because it needs more investigation, if they're relevant
- `hicpp-ignored-remove-result` is no longer enabled (removed because it's replaced by the already-enabled `bugprone-unused-return-value`

<!--
This PR template helps you to write a good pull request description.
Please feel free to include additional useful information even beyond what is requested below.

If your branch is on a personal fork and has a name that allows it to
run CI build/test jobs (e.g. "ci/foo"), remember to rename it BEFORE
opening the PR.  This avoids unnecessary redundant test runs. Renaming
the branch after opening the PR will close the PR.
https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/managing-branches-in-your-repository/renaming-a-branch
-->

## High Level Overview of Change

<!--
Please include a summary of the changes.
This may be a direct input to the release notes.
If too broad, please consider splitting into multiple PRs.
If a relevant task or issue, please link it here.
-->

I want our image to always produce the expected binary and not require external patching.
This would also mean that if someone builds locally in the CI image, they will not forget to patch the binary.

### Context of Change

In Clio, we package using cpack, and when I wanted to start using our images, I had to do it this way; otherwise, we would pack an unpatched binary.
And I think it's a good idea here as well, so backporting this change.

Also, on aarch64, libatomic is needed - we patch the binary now, and gtest discovery runs it right after it. libatomic was linked dynamically, which caused some issues. Instead, linking it statically explicitly simplifies the workflow.

`SANITIZERS_ENABLED` is moved to `CompilationEnv`, to make it easier to use in other modules.

body:

-->

A Sponsorship object lives in both the sponsor's (Owner) and the
sponsee's owner directories, but AccountDelete treated it as a deletion
blocker for both. It should be an obligation only for the sponsor, who
holds its reserve; for the sponsee it must be a non-obligation.
Otherwise anyone could block a sponsee's account deletion simply by
creating a Sponsorship.

related issue: https://github.com/XRPLF/rippled/issues/6892
